body:
### Why this matters

Azure Kubernetes Service Kubernetes 1.34 has a published AKS end-of-life date of November 30, 2026. Older Kubernetes versions receive fewer fixes, can move into reduced platform support, and can limit safe upgrade paths.

### What this control checks

This control finds clusters whose Kubernetes version starts with `1.34`. Upgrade the control plane, node pools, managed add-ons, and workload dependencies before the lifecycle date.

### Operational note

Treat Kubernetes upgrades as platform releases. Check deprecated APIs, admission policies, add-on compatibility, node images, PodDisruptionBudgets, and workload readiness before changing production clusters. The lifecycle date for this check is `2026-11-30`.
name: Ensure AKS clusters are not running Kubernetes 1.34
description: AKS clusters running Kubernetes 1.34 lifecycle version
timedControlSummary: AKS clusters on Kubernetes 1.34 should be upgraded before the published AKS lifecycle date.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Late AKS upgrades can limit supported upgrade paths, force rushed node pool replacement, and leave workloads on stale Kubernetes APIs.
remediationDescription:
## Azure remediation

> **Fleet-level upgrade (recommended):** upgrade AKS clusters by subscription, landing zone, or application environment. This reduces repeated compatibility work for Kubernetes APIs, add-ons, node pool images, Azure Policy, and workload identity.

### Azure Portal

1. Open the affected AKS cluster in the Azure portal.
2. Go to **Settings > Cluster configuration** or **Upgrade**.
3. Review available Kubernetes versions and upgrade insights.
4. Upgrade the control plane to a supported version such as `1.36`.
5. Upgrade each node pool and validate workloads after the nodes are replaced.

### Azure CLI

List available upgrades:

```bash
az aks get-upgrades \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <cluster-name> \
  --output table
```

Upgrade the cluster:

```bash
az aks upgrade \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <cluster-name> \
  --kubernetes-version 1.36 \
  --yes
```

Upgrade a node pool separately if needed:

```bash
az aks nodepool upgrade \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --cluster-name <cluster-name> \
  --name <nodepool-name> \
  --kubernetes-version 1.36
```

### References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/supported-kubernetes-versions
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-aks-cluster

timedControlRules.summary: AKS clusters on Kubernetes 1.34 should be upgraded before the published AKS lifecycle date.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late AKS upgrades can limit supported upgrade paths, force rushed node pool replacement, and leave workloads on stale Kubernetes APIs.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Upgrade the AKS cluster and node pools to Kubernetes 1.36 or another supported AKS version.
